docs.title: NormaDocs
docs.text: NormaDocs is a professional open-source tool that converts Markdown documents to academically-formatted DOCX and PDF files, with full support for major citation standards.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Multiple Citation Standards: APA 7th Edition, ICONTEC (NTC 1486), IEEE 8th Edition
  • Automatic Cover Pages: Title, author, institution, program, date extraction
  • Complete Formatting: Margins, typography, and spacing per selected standard
  • Bibliography Support: BibTeX (.bib) files and CSL styles via Pandoc
  • PDF Generation: LibreOffice or WeasyPrint
  • LanguageTool Integration: Grammar and spell checking

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
pip install normadocs

# Convert to APA (default)
normadocs document.md

# Convert to ICONTEC with PDF output
normadocs document.md --style icontec --format pdf

# Custom output directory
normadocs document.md -o ./Submissions -s apa -f all

docs.title: Supported Standards
docs.text: Standard Status Font Spacing Typical Use APA 7th Edition ✅ Complete Times New Roman 12pt Double Social Sciences ICONTEC (NTC 1486) ✅ Complete Arial 12pt 1.5 lines Colombian Academic IEEE 8th Edition ✅ Complete Times New Roman 10pt Single Engineering/Technical

docs.title: Contributing to NormaDocs
docs.text: Thank you for your interest in contributing!

docs.title: Development Setup
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/CristianMz21/normadocs.git
cd normadocs
pip install -e ".[dev]"

docs.title: Quality Standards
docs.text:
All contributions must pass:

make check  # lint + tests + coverage

docs.title: Code Style
docs.text:
• Python 3.10+
  • Google-style docstrings
  • Type hints required
  • 100 character line length

docs.title: Testing
docs.text:
make test        # Run tests
make test-cov    # With coverage

docs.title: Pull Request Process
docs.text:
1. Fork the repository
  2. Create a feature branch
  3. Make your changes
  4. Ensure all checks pass
  5. Submit a pull request

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.10 or higher
  • Pandoc (required for conversion)

docs.title: Install Pandoc
docs.text:
# Debian/Ubuntu
sudo apt install pandoc

# macOS
brew install pandoc

# Windows
# Download from https://pandoc.org/installing.html

docs.title: From PyPI
docs.text:
pip install normadocs

# With PDF support
pip install normadocs[pdf]

docs.title: From Source
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/CristianMz21/normadocs.git
cd normadocs
pip install -e ".[dev]"

docs.title: Verify Installation
docs.text:
normadocs --version
normadocs --help

docs.title: Troubleshooting Guide
docs.text: Common issues and solutions for NormaDocs.

docs.title: Pandoc Not Found
docs.text:
Symptom: Error: Pandoc no encontrado en el sistema

Solution: 

# Debian/Ubuntu
sudo apt install pandoc

# macOS
brew install pandoc

# Windows
# Download from https://pandoc.org/installing.html

Verify installation: 

pandoc --version

docs.title: PDF Generation Fails
docs.text:
Symptom: PDF is not generated, only DOCX

Solution: NormaDocs supports two PDF backends:

  1. LibreOffice (recommended): 

    sudo apt install libreoffice

  2. WeasyPrint (alternative): 

    pip install normadocs[pdf]

docs.title: Bibliography Not Working
docs.text:
Symptom: Citations appear as [?] in the output

Solution: 1. Ensure your .bib file is valid BibTeX format 2. Use the correct path to the CSL file 3. Check that citations in Markdown use correct keys: 

Se según [@author2024] demuestra...

docs.title: Encoding Issues
docs.text:
Symptom: Special characters appear wrong in output

Solution: - Ensure input files are UTF-8 encoded - Don't use "smart quotes" in Markdown source

docs.title: Common Error Messages
docs.text: Error Cause Solution FileNotFoundError Input file doesn't exist Check file path Pandoc conversion failed Pandoc error Check Markdown syntax LanguageTool server JAR not found LT not installed Install Java + LanguageTool

docs.title: Getting Help
docs.text: If you encounter an issue not covered here: 1. Check the GitHub Issues 2. Include your Markdown file content 3. Include output of normadocs --version

docs.title: Format and Syntax Reference
docs.text: Complete reference for all supported formats, characters, and special syntax in NormaDocs.

docs.title: Input Formats
docs.text: Format Extension Description Markdown .md Primary input format BibTeX .bib Bibliography/citation files CSL .csl Citation Style Language files

docs.title: Output Formats
docs.text: Format Generator Notes DOCX Pandoc Always generated PDF LibreOffice or WeasyPrint Optional via -f pdf or -f all

docs.title: YAML Frontmatter
docs.text: YAML frontmatter is the primary method for providing document metadata. It sits between --- delimiters at the top of the Markdown file.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text:
---
title: "Document Title"
author: "Author Name"
date: 2024
---

docs.title: Supported Fields
docs.text: Field Type Default Description title string "Untitled" Document title subtitle string None Optional subtitle author string None Author's name affiliation string None Department, institution program string None Academic program name ficha string None Course/sheet number (ERS format) institution string None University or institution name center string None Regional center name instructor string None Instructor/advisor name date string None Date (ISO format YYYY-MM-DD or year only) short_title string None Short title for APA running head author_id string None Student code (ICONTEC) location string None City, Country (ICONTEC) extra dict {} Any additional fields stored in metadata

docs.title: Example: APA 7 Document
docs.text:
---
title: "Effects of Machine Learning on Healthcare Outcomes"
subtitle: "A Systematic Review"
author: "Jane M. Smith"
affiliation: "Department of Computer Science, MIT"
program: "PhD in Health Informatics"
institution: "Massachusetts Institute of Technology"
date: 2024-04-15
short_title: "ML IN HEALTHCARE"
keywords:
  - machine learning
  - healthcare
  - systematic review
---

docs.title: Example: ICONTEC Document
docs.text:
---
title: "Título del Proyecto de Investigación"
subtitle: "Subtítulo si aplica"
author: "Nombre del Autor"
author_id: "Código de estudiante"
program: "Nombre del Programa Académico"
center: "Nombre del Centro Regional"
institution: "Nombre de la Universidad"
location: "Ciudad, País"
date: 2024
---

docs.title: Example: ERS Document (Legacy Format)
docs.text:
For documents without YAML frontmatter, the preprocessor extracts metadata from the first 14 lines:

**Título del Proyecto ERS**

Nombre del Autor

Nombre del Programa

Número de Ficha

Nombre de la Institución

Nombre del Centro

Fecha

docs.title: Page Breaks
docs.text:
Insert a page break using raw OpenXML blocks:

```{=openxml}
<w:p>
  <w:r>
    <w:br w:type="page"/>
  </w:r>
</w:p>
```

Page breaks are automatically inserted before every H1 (#) heading.

docs.title: Running Head (APA 7)
docs.text:
The running head appears in the left header. Enable it by setting short_title in frontmatter:

short_title: "SHORT TITLE IN UPPERCASE"

The running head consists of "SHORT TITLE: " prefix followed by the short title in uppercase.

docs.title: Keywords
docs.text:
Keywords in the document body use this format:

**Keywords:** word1, word2, word3

or in Spanish (ICONTEC):

**Palabras clave:** word1, word2, word3

docs.title: Citations (BibTeX)
docs.text:
Use Pandoc-style citations with a BibTeX file:

The study found that... [@smith2024].

Multiple sources state this [@smith2024; @jones2023].

docs.title: Table of Contents (TOC) Entries
docs.text:
Lines in the format 1. ... ... 5 (with dots and page number) are automatically escaped to prevent Pandoc from converting them to ordered lists:

Resumen ..................................................... 3
Objetivos ................................................... 4

These become:

1\. Resumen ..................................................... 3
2\. Objetivos ................................................... 4

docs.title: Multiline Tables
docs.text:
Pandoc multiline tables (dashed-line format) are automatically converted to pipe tables for better compatibility:

Input (Pandoc multiline):

+------------+------------+
| Header 1   | Header 2   |
+============+============+
| Cell 1     | Cell 2     |
+------------+------------+

Output (pipe table):

| Header 1 | Header 2 |
| --- | --- |
| Cell 1 | Cell 2 |

docs.title: Box-Drawing Characters
docs.text:
ASCII art using box-drawing characters is preserved:

┌─────────────────┐
│                 │
│   Content here  │
│                 │
└─────────────────┘

docs.title: Supported Characters
docs.text: NormaDocs supports full UTF-8 character encoding including:

docs.title: Spanish Characters
docs.text: Character Example ÁÉÍÓÚ Ü Ángeles, Pérez, María áéíóú ü cámara, número, mínimo Ñ España, Niño ñ araña, dueño ¡ ! ¡Advertencia!, ¡Hola! ¿ ? ¿Qué tal?

docs.title: Latin-1 Supplement
docs.text:
All Latin-1 (ISO 8859-1) characters are supported, including:

  • French: àâäçéèêëîïôùûüÿœæ
  • German: äöüß
  • Portuguese: ãõñç
  • Italian: àèéìíòóù
  • Scandinavian: åæøÅÆØ

docs.title: Special Symbols
docs.text:
• Degree symbol: °
  • Plus-minus: ±
  • Multiply: ×
  • Divide: ÷
  • Bullet: •
  • Trademark: ™ ® ©
  • Currency: € £ ¥ $

docs.title: Math Symbols
docs.text:
• Greek letters: α β γ δ ε λ μ π σ Ω
  • Operators: ≤ ≥ ≠ ≈ ∞ ± − × ÷
  • Sets: ∈ ∉ ⊂ ⊃ ∪ ∩

docs.title: Code Image Generation (Optional)
docs.text: NormaDocs can transform code blocks into beautifully styled images for academic documents. This uses the monokai theme by default with syntax highlighting.

docs.title: Syntax
docs.text:
Add {code} after the language identifier to mark a code block for image conversion:

```python {code}
def hello():
    print("Hello, World!")

### Supported Languages

Pygments supports 300+ languages including: `python`, `javascript`, `typescript`, `java`, `c`, `cpp`, `csharp`, `go`, `rust`, `ruby`, `php`, `swift`, `kotlin`, `scala`, `sql`, `html`, `css`, `json`, `yaml`, `xml`, `bash`, `shell`, `markdown`, `latex`, and many more.

### How It Works

1. **Detection**: The preprocessor identifies `{code}` marked blocks
2. **Highlighting**: Pygments generates styled HTML with syntax highlighting
3. **Rendering**: imgkit converts the HTML to a PNG image
4. **Replacement**: The code block is replaced with a markdown image reference
5. **Embedding**: Pandoc embeds the image in the final DOCX

### Installation

```bash
pip install normadocs[codeimage]

This installs pygments>=2.17.0 and imgkit>=1.0.0.

Note: imgkit requires wkhtmltopdf system library: 

sudo apt install wkhtmltopdf      # Debian/Ubuntu
sudo dnf install wkhtmltopdf      # Fedora
brew install wkhtmltopdf          # macOS

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
Input:

# Results

The implementation follows this pattern:

```python {code}
class DataProcessor:
    def __init__(self, config: dict):
        self.config = config
        self.cache = {}

    def process(self, data: list) -> list:
        return [self.transform(item) for item in data]
```

Output: A styled PNG image with syntax-highlighted Python code embedded in the DOCX.

docs.title: Theme
docs.text: Code images use the monokai theme (dark background with colorful syntax highlighting). This provides excellent contrast and readability for printed documents.

docs.title: File Output
docs.text:
Generated images are saved to ./code_images/ directory next to the output DOCX, with names like: - code_image_001_python_a1b2c3d4.png - code_image_002_javascript_e5f6g7h8.png

Images are cached based on content hash, so repeated conversions of the same code don't regenerate the image.

docs.title: Hard-Wrapped Line Joining
docs.text:
The preprocessor joins consecutive lines that were hard-wrapped at approximately 72 characters into single paragraphs. This is transparent to the user.

Before: 

This is a very long paragraph that was originally
wrapped at 72 characters and needs to be joined
back together for proper formatting.

After: 

This is a very long paragraph that was originally wrapped at 72 characters and needs to be joined back together for proper formatting.

docs.title: HTML Entity Encoding
docs.text:
The # character in titles is encoded as # to prevent Pandoc from interpreting it as a heading marker:

Title with # hashtag

becomes safe content.

docs.title: OpenXML Blocks
docs.text:
Raw OpenXML can be inserted using the openxml output format:

```{=openxml}
<w:p>
  <w:r>
    <w:br w:type="page"/>
  </w:r>
</w:p>
```

docs.title: Attributes on Blocks
docs.text:
Pandoc attributes syntax is supported for images and other elements:

![Caption](image.png){width=50%}

docs.title: LanguageTool Integration
docs.text:
NormaDocs can check grammar using LanguageTool:

# Basic check
normadocs document.md --language-tool

# Strict mode (fail on any error)
normadocs document.md --language-tool --lt-strict

# With Docker (auto-starts container)
normadocs document.md --language-tool --lt-docker

# Save errors to file
normadocs document.md --language-tool --lt-report errors.json

docs.title: Standards Comparison
docs.text: Feature APA 7th ICONTEC IEEE 8th Body font Times New Roman 12pt Arial 12pt Times New Roman 10pt Line spacing Double 1.5 Single Top/bottom margins 1" 3cm 1" Left margin 1" 3cm 1" Right margin 1" 2cm 1" Table caption prefix Table Tabla Table Figure caption prefix Figure Figura Fig. Running head Yes No No Abstract page No Yes No Keywords section Yes Yes Yes

docs.title: Supported Standards
docs.text: NormaDocs supports multiple academic formatting standards.

docs.title: APA 7th Edition
docs.text:
American Psychological Association format, commonly used in social sciences.

Features: - Times New Roman 12pt - Double line spacing (2.0) - 1-inch (2.54cm) margins all sides - Running head: short title in uppercase (left header), page number (right header) - Cover page, abstract, keywords sections - 5 heading levels with specific formatting - First-line indent in body paragraphs - Block quotes with left border - Tables with horizontal borders only - Figures with label + title above, nota below

Configuration: apa7.yaml

Example Document: examples/example_apa.md

See APA Configuration →

docs.title: ICONTEC (NTC 1486)
docs.text:
Colombian technical and academic standard.

Features: - Arial 12pt - 1.5 line spacing - 3cm margins (top, bottom, left), 4cm (right) - Cover page with specific structure

Configuration: icontec.yaml

See ICONTEC Configuration →

docs.title: IEEE 8th Edition
docs.text:
Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers format.

Features: - Times New Roman 10pt - Single line spacing - 1-inch margins - Page numbers in headers

Configuration: ieee.yaml

See IEEE Configuration →

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The APA 7th Edition standard in NormaDocs applies automatic formatting to produce documents that comply with the American Psychological Association's 7th Edition guidelines for academic writing.

docs.title: Default Settings
docs.text:
name: "APA 7th Edition"
version: "7.0"
citation_style: apa

fonts:
  body:
    name: "Times New Roman"
    size: 12
  headings:
    name: "Times New Roman"
    level1:
      alignment: center
      bold: true
    level2:
      alignment: left
      bold: true
    level3:
      alignment: left
      bold: true
      italic: true

margins:
  unit: inches
  top: 1
  bottom: 1
  left: 1
  right: 1

spacing:
  line: double
  paragraph_before: 0
  paragraph_after: 0

page_setup:
  page_numbers: true
  header: true
  first_page_number: 1

tables:
  borders: horizontal_only
  caption_prefix: "Table"
  caption_above: true
  note_suffix: "Author's elaboration."
  vertical_align: top

figures:
  caption_prefix: "Figure"
  title_above: true
  nota_prefix: "Nota."

running_head:
  enabled: true
  max_length: 50

docs.title: Customization
docs.text:
Override defaults by passing a config dictionary to the formatter:

from normadocs.formatters import get_formatter

formatter = get_formatter("apa", "document.docx", config={
    "fonts": {"body": {"name": "Arial", "size": 11}},
    "margins": {"top": 1.5, "bottom": 1.5, "left": 1.25, "right": 1.25}
})

docs.title: Font
docs.text:
• Body text: Times New Roman, 12pt
  • Headings: Times New Roman (see Heading Levels below)
  • No other fonts are permitted in the main text

docs.title: Line Spacing
docs.text:
• Body text: Double spacing throughout
  • Tables: Single spacing within cells
  • References: Double spacing between entries, single spacing within entries
  • Block quotes: Double spacing (0.5 inch indent on left)

docs.title: Margins
docs.text: • 1 inch on all sides (top, bottom, left, right)

docs.title: Page Numbers
docs.text:
• Position: Top right of every page
  • Format: Arabic numerals (1, 2, 3...)
  • First page number: 1
  • Font: Times New Roman 12pt

docs.title: Running Head
docs.text:
• Left header: Short title in ALL CAPS (maximum 50 characters)
  • Right header: Page number
  • Cover page: No running head appears
  • Pages 2+: Running head visible

The short title is extracted from the short_title field in YAML frontmatter metadata. If not provided, the running head is not added.

Configuration: To disable the running head, set running_head.enabled: false in the config:

running_head:
  enabled: false

docs.title: Heading Levels (APA 7th Edition)
docs.text:
APA 7th Edition uses five levels of headings:

Level Format Example Level 1 Centered, Bold, Title Case Introduction Level 2 Left-aligned, Bold, Title Case Literature Review Level 3 Left-aligned, Bold, Italic, Title Case Theoretical Framework Level 4 Indented 0.5in, Bold, Title Case, ends with period Sample. The sample consisted of... Level 5 Indented 0.5in, Bold, Italic, Title Case, ends with period Procedure. Data were collected...

Note: APA 7th Edition does not use numbered headings (1, 1.1, 1.1.1). Use only the five levels above.

docs.title: Cover Page
docs.text:
The cover page includes (centered, in this order):

  1. Title (bold, centered in upper half of page)
  2. Author name (centered)
  3. Institutional affiliation (centered)
  4. Course number (if applicable)
  5. Instructor name (if applicable)
  6. Date (centered)

docs.title: Example Frontmatter (YAML)
docs.text:
---
title: "The Effects of Machine Learning on Higher Education Outcomes"
short_title: "Machine Learning Effects on Education"
author: "Sarah M. Johnson"
affiliation: "Department of Computer Science, State University"
institution: "State University"
date: "2026-04-10"
---

docs.title: Abstract
docs.text:
• Starts on page 2 (after cover page)
  • Heading "Abstract" centered, bold
  • Maximum 250 words (student papers) / 150 words (professional journals)
  • No paragraph indent
  • Keywords section follows immediately after abstract

docs.title: Keywords
docs.text:
• Label "Keywords:" in italics
  • Keywords in regular text, left-indented 0.5 inches
  • Separated by commas

Example: 

Keywords: machine learning, higher education, adaptive learning, educational technology

docs.title: First-Line Indent
docs.text:
• 0.5 inch first-line indent on all paragraphs
  • Exception: First paragraph after a heading has NO indent

docs.title: Alignment
docs.text:
• Left-aligned (default)
  • Do not justify text

docs.title: Paragraph Spacing
docs.text:
• Double spacing throughout
  • No additional space before or after paragraphs

docs.title: References
docs.text:
• Start on new page after body
  • Heading "References" centered, bold
  • Alphabetical order by author's last name
  • Hanging indent: 0.5 inch (first line flush left, subsequent lines indented)
  • Double spacing between entries, single spacing within entries
  • No heading styles within references section

docs.title: Example Entry
docs.text: Smith, A. B., & Jones, C. D. (2024). Title of the article. Journal Name, 45(2), 112-130. https://doi.org/10.0000/journal.2024.0001

docs.title: Caption Format
docs.text:
• "Table N" in bold, followed by title in italics
  • Caption appears ABOVE the table
  • Table number increments sequentially

Example: 

Table 1
*Student Performance Metrics by Intervention Type*

docs.title: Formatting
docs.text:
• Centered on page
  • Horizontal borders only (no vertical lines)
  • Header row in bold
  • Single spacing within cells
  • Notes below table in italics, starting with "Nota."

docs.title: Note Example
docs.text: Nota. Effect sizes represent Cohen's d values. CI = confidence interval.

docs.title: Caption Format
docs.text:
• Caption appears BELOW the figure
  • "Figure N" in bold, followed by title in italics
  • Notes in italics, starting with "Nota."

Example: 

Figure 1
*Student Engagement Trends Over Academic Year*

Nota. Data collected from Fall 2024 cohort (n = 245).

docs.title: Formatting
docs.text:
• Centered on page
  • Image scaled to fit page width (max 6.5 inches)
  • High resolution recommended

docs.title: Block Quotes
docs.text:
• Indented 0.5 inch on left
  • Double spaced (same as body text)
  • No quotation marks
  • Attribution on new line below quote

Example: 

Smith (2024) explained this phenomenon:

    The implementation of adaptive learning systems requires
    significant institutional investment in both technology
    and faculty development.

(Smith, 2024, p. 45)

docs.title: Implemented Features
docs.text:
The following APA 7th Edition features are implemented in NormaDocs:

  • Cover page with title, author, institutional affiliation
  • 1-inch margins on all sides
  • Times New Roman 12pt font
  • Double line spacing
  • Page numbers (top right)
  • Running head (short title left, page number right)
  • Heading styles (5 levels)
  • First-line indent (0.5 inch) for body paragraphs
  • Abstract/Keywords formatting
  • Table captions ("Table N")
  • Table formatting (horizontal borders only)
  • Table notes ("Nota.")
  • Figure captions ("Figure N")
  • Hanging indent for references
  • Foreign word italics (e.g., ad hoc, in vitro)
  • Page breaks before Conclusions and References sections

docs.title: Missing Features
docs.text:
The following APA 7th Edition features are NOT yet implemented:

  • Author notes (footnotes on cover page)
  • DOI formatting in references
  • Appendices formatting
  • Block quote formatting (indentation)
  • Seriation within text (APA bulleted lists)
  • Secondary temporal derivatives (e.g., "running heads are right-aligned" not implemented)

docs.title: Example Document
docs.text: A complete example APA 7th Edition document is available at examples/example_apa.md.

docs.title: Example Usage
docs.text:
# Convert APA document
normadocs examples/example_apa.md -s apa -o ./output

# With PDF output
normadocs examples/example_apa.md -s apa -f all -o ./output

docs.title: Python Library Usage
docs.text:
from normadocs.preprocessor import MarkdownPreprocessor
from normadocs.pandoc_client import PandocRunner
from normadocs.formatters import get_formatter

# Process markdown
processor = MarkdownPreprocessor()
clean_md, meta = processor.process(input_markdown)

# Convert to DOCX
PandocRunner().run(clean_md, "output.docx")

# Apply APA formatting
formatter = get_formatter("apa", "output.docx")
formatter.process(meta)
formatter.save("output_apa.docx")

docs.title: ICONTEC NTC 1486 Standard
docs.text: Complete reference for the ICONTEC NTC 1486 Colombian academic standard.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
ICONTEC (Instituto Colombiano de Normas Técnicas y Certificación) is the Colombian organization responsible for technical standards. The NTC 1486 standard defines the formatting rules for academic and technical documents in Colombia.

NormaDocs supports ICONTEC NTC 1486 for scientific and research documents.

docs.title: Default Configuration
docs.text:
name: "ICONTEC (NTC 1486)"
version: "1.0"
citation_style: icontec

fonts:
  body:
    name: "Arial"
    size: 12
  headings:
    name: "Arial"
    level1:
      alignment: center
      bold: true
      uppercase: true

margins:
  unit: cm
  top: 3
  bottom: 2
  left: 3
  right: 2

spacing:
  line: 1.5
  paragraph_before: 0
  paragraph_after: 0

page_setup:
  page_numbers: true
  header: false
  first_page_number: 1

tables:
  borders: full
  caption_prefix: "Tabla"
  caption_above: true

figures:
  caption_prefix: "Figura"

docs.title: Typography
docs.text: Element Font Size Style Body text Arial 12pt Normal Headings Arial 12pt Bold, Uppercase for Level 1 Captions Arial 11pt Normal

docs.title: Spacing
docs.text:
• Line spacing: 1.5 throughout the document
  • Paragraph spacing: No additional space before or after paragraphs
  • Margins: 3cm (top, bottom, left), 2cm (right)

docs.title: Page Layout
docs.text:
• Page numbers appear in the footer, centered
  • No header on any page (including first page)
  • First page number: 1
  • No running head

docs.title: Tables
docs.text:
• Borders: Full grid borders on all tables
  • Caption position: Above the table
  • Caption prefix: "Tabla" (e.g., "Tabla 1. Descripción del sistema")
  • Caption style: Normal weight, left-aligned caption

docs.title: Figures
docs.text:
• Caption position: Below the figure
  • Caption prefix: "Figura" (e.g., "Figura 1. Arquitectura del sistema")
  • Caption style: Normal weight, left-aligned caption

docs.title: Cover Page
docs.text:
The cover page is generated automatically from YAML frontmatter metadata. The structure includes:

[Title - centered, bold, uppercase]
[Subtitle if present]
[Blank line]
[Author Name]
[Author ID / Student Code]
[Program Name]
[Regional Center Name]
[University Name]
[Location (City, Country)]
[Date - Year only]

docs.title: Required Sections
docs.text:
ICONTEC documents typically follow this structure:

  1. Resumen (Abstract) - 150-250 words, single paragraph, no indentation
  2. Introducción (Introduction) - Context and justification (no heading title)
  3. Marco Teórico (Theoretical Framework)
  4. Antecedentes (Background)
  5. Planteamiento del Problema (Problem Statement)
  6. Metodología (Methodology)
  7. Diseño de Investigación (Research Design)
  8. Población y Muestra (Population and Sample)
  9. Instrumentos de Recolección de Datos (Data Collection Instruments)
  10. Resultados (Results)
  11. Discusión (Discussion)
  12. Conclusiones (Conclusions)
  13. Recomendaciones (Recommendations)
  14. Referencias (References)
  15. Anexos (Annexes) - Supplementary materials

docs.title: Keywords
docs.text:
Keywords appear after the abstract with the format:

**Palabras clave:** word1, word2, word3

docs.title: YAML Frontmatter Fields
docs.text:
ICONTEC uses these specific frontmatter fields:

---
title: "Research Project Title"
subtitle: "Subtitle if applicable"
author: "Author Name"
author_id: "Student Code"
program: "Academic Program Name"
center: "Regional Center Name"
institution: "University Name"
location: "City, Country"
date: 2024
---
Field Description Required title Document title Yes subtitle Optional subtitle No author Author's full name Yes author_id Student code or ID Yes program Academic program name Yes center Regional center name Yes institution University name Yes location City and country Yes date Year of submission Yes

docs.title: Reference Format
docs.text: ICONTEC uses a specific reference format:

docs.title: Book
docs.text: Author, A. A., & Author, B. B. (Year). Title of book in italics. Publisher.

docs.title: Journal Article
docs.text: Author, A. A., & Author, B. B. (Year). Article title. Journal Name, Volume(Number), pages. https://doi.org/xxxxx

docs.title: Online Resource
docs.text: Author, A. A. (Year). Title of page. Website Name. https://url.com

docs.title: Comparison with APA 7
docs.text: Aspect APA 7th ICONTEC NTC 1486 Body font Times New Roman 12pt Arial 12pt Heading font Times New Roman Arial Line spacing Double 1.5 Top/bottom margins 1" (2.54cm) 3cm Left margin 1" 3cm Right margin 1" 2cm Table caption Above, "Table X." Above, "Tabla X." Figure caption Below, "Figure X." Below, "Figura X." Running head Yes (left header) No Abstract language English Spanish

docs.title: CLI Usage
docs.text:
# Convert Markdown to ICONTEC DOCX
normadocs document.md -s icontec

# Generate ICONTEC PDF
normadocs document.md -s icontec -f all

# With abstract keywords
normadocs document.md -s icontec --language-tool

docs.title: Default Configuration
docs.text:
fonts:
  body:
    name: "Times New Roman"
    size: 10
margins:
  unit: inches
  top: 1.0
  bottom: 1.0
  left: 1.0
  right: 1.0
spacing:
  line: single

docs.title: Formatting Rules
docs.text:
• Font: Times New Roman 10pt
  • Line spacing: Single
  • Margins: 1 inch all sides
  • Page numbers: Top right header
  • Columns: Two-column layout for final paper (not implemented)

docs.title: CLI Reference
docs.text: Complete reference for the normadocs command-line interface.

docs.title: normadocs convert
docs.text:
Converts a Markdown file to a formatted DOCX/PDF.

normadocs convert INPUT_FILE [OPTIONS]

Arguments: | Argument | Description | |----------|-------------| | INPUT_FILE | Path to input Markdown file (required) |

Options: | Option | Short | Description | Default | |--------|-------|-------------|---------| | --output-dir | -o | Output directory | ./output | | --format | -f | Output format: docx, pdf, or all | docx | | --style | -s | Citation style: apa, icontec, ieee | apa | | --bibliography | -b | BibTeX file for citations | - | | --csl | -c | CSL style file | - | | --language-tool | - | Enable LanguageTool checking | false | | --lt-strict | - | Strict LanguageTool mode | false |

Examples: 

# Basic conversion (APA style)
normadocs document.md

# ICONTEC style with PDF
normadocs document.md -s icontec -f all

# With bibliography
normadocs document.md -b refs.bib -c apa.csl

# With LanguageTool
normadocs document.md --language-tool

docs.title: normadocs --version
docs.text:
Shows the installed version.

normadocs --version

docs.title: normadocs --help
docs.text:
Shows help information.

normadocs --help

docs.title: Library Usage
docs.text: You can use NormaDocs programmatically in your Python applications.

docs.title: Basic Usage
docs.text:
from normadocs.preprocessor import MarkdownPreprocessor
from normadocs.pandoc_client import PandocRunner
from normadocs.formatters import get_formatter

# 1. Preprocess Markdown
processor = MarkdownPreprocessor()
md_content = open("document.md").read()
clean_md, meta = processor.process(md_content)

# 2. Convert to DOCX
runner = PandocRunner()
runner.run(clean_md, "output.docx")

# 3. Format with APA style
formatter = get_formatter("apa", "output.docx")
formatter.process(meta)
formatter.save("output_final.docx")

docs.title: With Bibliography
docs.text: runner.run(clean_md, "output.docx", bibliography="refs.bib", csl="apa.csl")

docs.title: Custom Configuration
docs.text:
custom_config = {
    "fonts": {"body": {"name": "Arial", "size": 12}},
    "margins": {"top": 1.5, "bottom": 1.5}
}
formatter = get_formatter("apa", "doc.docx", config=custom_config)

docs.title: Available Standards
docs.text: Standard String APA 7th Edition "apa" ICONTEC NTC 1486 "icontec" IEEE 8th Edition "ieee"

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: See the source code docstrings for complete API documentation.
